requiredFiles.reason: Update the response-details state setter so the existing toggle button's aria-expanded value matches the panel's open/hidden state for both toggle and close actions without changing watch behavior.
Add an independently runnable unittest regression using tests.frontend_harness.run_dashboard to verify response-details visibility and accessibility state synchronization.

acceptanceCriteria: The response-details panel can still open and close visually.
The existing response-details button's aria-expanded state is synchronized when the panel opens and when it closes, so assistive technology receives the correct expanded/collapsed state.
The response-details toggle opens a hidden panel and closes an open panel.
The response-details close button closes the panel and returns the toggle's aria-expanded state to false.
Existing watch behavior remains unchanged: clicking the watch button toggles data-watching and the watch-status text between Watching incident and Not watching.
The regression test is placed under tests/generated/test_*.py, uses the standard-library unittest runner, and imports the existing tests.frontend_harness.run_dashboard helper.
implementationChecklist: Make setDetailsOpen set detailsPanel.hidden to the inverse of the requested open state and set #detailsToggle aria-expanded to the requested state as a string.
Continue routing both the toggle click and details-close click through setDetailsOpen so visual and accessibility state cannot diverge.
Leave the watch button's dataset.watching toggle and watch-status text behavior unchanged.
Create a unittest that verifies opening via #detailsToggle yields detailsHidden false and detailsExpanded true.
Verify closing via a second toggle click yields detailsHidden true and detailsExpanded false.
Verify closing via #detailsClose after opening also yields detailsHidden true and detailsExpanded false.
Include watch assertions confirming its existing state and status behavior still work through the frontend harness.
verificationChecklist: Run the new unittest module independently.
Confirm a toggle-open action reports detailsHidden as false and detailsExpanded as true.
Confirm both toggle-close and close-button actions report detailsHidden as true and detailsExpanded as false.
Confirm watch activation reports watching true and Watching incident, and a subsequent activation returns it to false and Not watching.
